notes: This skill's value depends on live Jira access and a real, investigable codebase, so these prompts use bracketed placeholders instead of real tickets/repos/companies. Before running an eval pass, substitute a real ticket URL, reference comment URL, and codebase path(s) that the runner actually has access to — pick something non-sensitive (e.g. a sandbox project, or a real bug you're comfortable an AI-assisted comment landing on). Do not commit the substituted, filled-in prompts back into this file if the ticket/company details are sensitive; keep the placeholders here and substitute only in the ephemeral eval run.

evals.prompt: Can you triage <JIRA_TICKET_URL>? Look into why this is happening and post your analysis as a comment on the ticket. The codebase(s) that implement this feature are at <CODEBASE_ROOT_1> and <CODEBASE_ROOT_2>.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches the full ticket (description + all comments), scopes the investigation to the given repos, delegates code investigation to a subagent, verifies the subagent's key claims directly before writing anything, drafts a root-cause analysis using the default template (no reference style was given), and posts it to the ticket automatically (no dry-run requested).

evals.prompt: Please triage <JIRA_TICKET_URL> and post an analysis comment similar in style to what I did here: <REFERENCE_COMMENT_URL>. The relevant code is in <CODEBASE_ROOT>.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches both the target ticket and the reference comment, extracts the reference's structural pattern (opening marker, section headers, tone), and produces a posted comment whose shape mirrors the reference rather than the generic default template — while the actual root-cause content is still independently investigated and verified against the given codebase.

evals.prompt: Investigate <JIRA_TICKET_URL> and tell me what you think is going on — don't post anything yet, I want to review it first. Codebase is at <CODEBASE_ROOT>.
evals.expected_output: The skill treats this as a dry-run: it performs the full investigation and verification, then shows the complete drafted comment (and/or saves it to a file) without ever calling addCommentToJiraIssue.
